item.title: Heavy Rain Warning in force for Fiji
item.description: A Heavy Rain Warning is now in force for Fiji as an active trough of low pressure gradually moves eastwards towards the country. The Nadi Weather Office says cloud and rain associated with the system are already affecting parts of Fiji and are expected to continue impacting the group until later tomorrow.
item.content:encoded:
A Heavy Rain Warning is now in force for Fiji as an active trough of low pressure gradually moves eastwards towards the country.

The Nadi Weather Office says cloud and rain associated with the system are already affecting parts of Fiji and are expected to continue impacting the group until later tomorrow.

They say occasional rain will become frequent and heavy from later today, accompanied by thunderstorms.

The Weather Office says conditions are expected to gradually improve in the western parts of the country by around midday tomorrow.

They say heavy rainfall may lead to flash flooding in flood-prone and low-lying areas, including informal settlements, businesses and Irish crossings, which could result in traffic disruptions.

There is also a risk of surface flooding on streets in urban areas with poor drainage systems.

item.title: Taveuni born Harold Nair to appear on fijivillage Straight Talk after undergoing one of the most advanced brain surgeries ever performed in WA
item.description: Taveuni born Harold Nair to appear on fijivillage Straight Talk after undergoing one of the most advanced brain surgeries ever performed in WA

item.description: FICAC has asked for more time in their case against Director of Public Prosecutions Christopher Pryde to serve his relevant docume

item.title: 26 lives lost in road accidents this year
item.description: It is tragic that 26 lives have been lost on our roads this year, compared to 29 deaths recorded during the same period last year.

item.title: Palliative care not an optional add-on in cervical cancer response - Dr Cikamatana
item.description: Palliative care is an essential component of Fiji's response to cervical cancer and should not be viewed as an optional add-on.The

item.title: Four officers sent on leave after investigation into Radravu's arrest
Ministry dismisses claims of changes to school operations, Term 3 to proceed as scheduled
item.description: Schools across Fiji will continue to operate as normal in Term 3, with the Ministry of Education confirming that there have been no changes to the 2026 academic calendar, student attendance requirements, teaching programmes or national examinations. Permanent Secretary for the Ministry of Education Navin Raj issued this clarification this evening following media reports and speculation regarding a
item.content:encoded:
Schools across Fiji will continue to operate as normal in Term 3, with the Ministry of Education confirming that there have been no changes to the 2026 academic calendar, student attendance requirements, teaching programmes or national examinations.

Permanent Secretary for the Ministry of Education Navin Raj issued this clarification this evening following media reports and speculation regarding an internal administrative circular, assuring students, parents, teachers and other stakeholders that Term 3 will begin on September 7th, and conclude on December 4th as scheduled.

Raj says Year 8, Year 12 and Year 13 external examinations will proceed according to the approved assessment calendar, while all students are expected to attend classes physically throughout the term.

He says teaching and learning activities will continue uninterrupted, reflecting the Ministry's commitment to delivering quality education.

The Permanent Secretary also clarified that the completion of syllabus coverage during Term 2 is a long-standing practice, with Term 3 traditionally focused on trial examinations, revision classes, remedial programmes and preparation for external examinations.

He highlighted that since 2024, the Fiji Year 8 Examination has been conducted during Term 3, with results released in November to allow secondary schools to enrol Year 9 students based on official national examination results rather than internal assessment marks.

Raj also addressed concerns regarding the "Student Home Life" field in the Fiji Education Management Information System (FEMIS), saying it is a standard component of student records used to support planning, student welfare, policy development, resource allocation and targeted interventions.

He says schools have been reminded to ensure that all student information is accurately maintained and regularly updated within the FEMIS platform.

He further noted that Term 3 remains an important period for continuous assessments in Years 1 to 3, annual examinations for Years 4 to 7 and Years 9 to 11, as well as pastoral care programmes and activities aimed at supporting students' holistic development.

Year 10 students will also participate in career expos designed to help them make informed decisions about subject selections before entering Year 11.

He stresses that the circular referred to in recent reports is an internal administrative communication intended for Heads of Schools and relevant Ministry personnel and does not introduce any changes to educational programmes or normal school operations.

item.title: Four killed after vehicle plunges into river in Vatukoula
item.description: Four people have died following a road accident in Vatukoula this morning. Police say the people were returning from a family function in Ba when the driver, a man in his 40s, lost control of the vehicle.
item.content:encoded:
Four people have died following a road accident in Vatukoula this morning.

Police say the people were returning from a family function in Ba when the driver, a man in his 40s, lost control of the vehicle.

The vehicle veered off the road and plunged into a nearby river.

The driver, a 43-year-old man, his wife, their nine-year-old daughter and his sister-in-law were rushed to Tavua Hospital, where their deaths were confirmed by medical officials.

Police investigations into the circumstances surrounding the accident are continuing.

item.description: Unity Fiji Leader Savenaca Narube says the Constitution Review Commission and other such comissions should be stopped. He has high
